name: Norma's Turkey Alla Lombarda
recipeIngredient: 8 cups Soft breadcrumbs or unseasoned croutons
2 cups Cooking apples, peeled and chopped
1 1/2 cups Chablis or other dry white wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove giblets and neck from turkey; rinse and place in a small saucepan with 3 cups water. Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
2. Remove giblets and neck from broth, reserving the broth. Set aside and let cool.
3. Combine breadcrumbs and next 4 ingredients (poultry seasoning, oregano, pepper, and Italian sausage) in a large bowl, stirring well. Set aside.
4. Remove sausage from casing. Cook sausage in a large skillet over high heat until it browns, stirring to crumble the meat. Remove meat with a slotted spoon, reserving pan drippings in the skillet. Add sausage to breadcrumb mixture; stir well.
5. Add onion to pan drippings and cook over medium heat until golden. Remove with a slotted spoon and stir into breadcrumb mixture.
6. Combine apples, chestnuts, and pine nuts. Add to breadcrumb mixture, stirring well.
7. Remove meat from reserved turkey neck; coarsely chop the meat and giblets. Stir meat and giblets into breadcrumb mixture.
8. Combine eggs and 1 cup of reserved broth; stir well. Pour over breadcrumb mixture and stir well.
9. Rinse turkey with cold water; pat dry. Stuff the dressing into the body cavities of the turkey. If excess skin around the tail has been cut away, tuck legs under the flap of skin around the tail. If skin is intact, close the cavity with skewers and truss. Tie ends of legs to the tail with cord.
10. Lift wingtips up and over back; tuck under the bird.
11. Spoon remaining dressing into a greased 11 x 7 x 2-inch baking dish. Cover and chill.
12. Place turkey in a shallow roasting pan, breast side up; rub the bird with crushed rosemary. Pour wine into roasting pan around the bird.
13. Insert meat thermometer in the meaty part of the turkey thigh, making sure it does not touch bone. Bake at 325°F until the meat thermometer reaches 185°F. If the turkey starts to brown too much, cover loosely with aluminum foil.
14. When the turkey is two-thirds done, cut the cord or band holding the drumstick ends to the tail. This will ensure that the thighs are cooked internally. The turkey is done when the drumsticks are easily moved up and down. Let stand 15 minutes before carving.
15. Bake remaining dressing at 350°F for 30 to 40 minutes.
